authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 149.610 - Requirements for provision of good faith estimates of expected charges for uninsured or self-pay individuals
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulatory text for healthcare good faith estimate requirements, making it directly authoritative for estimate artifact and dispute-boundary handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: healthcare estimate package validation and estimate-disposition follow-up
used_for: estimate version and itemized expected charge handling
dispute and non-owned legal boundary posture

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA privacy regulator. The guidance constrains PHI use and disclosure in estimate follow-up notes, vendor routing, callback logs, and patient/member artifacts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI-limited follow-up artifact and communication routing

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS prior authorization guidance is authoritative for payer/provider authorization workflow expectations and supports escalation when estimate follow-up is blocked by authorization or payer-data status.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: authorization, referral, and eligibility prerequisite handling
used_for: patient access and utilization management routing

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Benefit Policy Manual, Chapter 7 - Home Health Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS manual guidance is authoritative for Medicare home health coverage prerequisites, homebound and plan-of-care dependencies, and the need to avoid clinical or medical-necessity determinations in administrative follow-up.
used_for: medical necessity and clinical judgment refusal

authoritative_sources.title: Sample Good Faith Estimate for Uninsured (or Self-Pay) Individuals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS provides the public sample estimate form and expected fields for provider, service address, codes, quantities, expected costs, separately scheduled services, disclaimers, and dispute process references.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: estimate artifact terminology and evidence shape
used_for: output shape for field-service follow-up summaries
separately scheduled service and co-provider evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: United States Core Data for Interoperability Version 6
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTP/ONC USCDI is the federal healthcare interoperability data baseline. It supports patient, encounter, order, care team, procedure, and clinical note terminology used in EHR, CRM, and operational estimate follow-up records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: system-of-record terminology and tenant term normalization

authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 429 - Rule Concerning Cooling-off Period for Sales Made at Homes or at Certain Other Locations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public contractor and home-services consumer guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal trade regulation for covered in-home sales and cancellation rights, directly relevant when home-service estimate follow-up touches agreement, cancellation, or in-home sales posture.

authoritative_sources.title: Buyer's Remorse: The FTC's Cooling-Off Rule May Help
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public contractor and home-services consumer guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the federal consumer-protection regulator. The guidance explains in-home sales, cancellation forms, refund timing, and exceptions that estimate follow-up must preserve and route rather than decide.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: consumer-facing agreement exception and cancellation escalation

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Required prerequisites include tenant service model, jurisdiction, business unit, service setting, source hierarchy, RACI, escalation SLA, estimate ID/version, agreement or consent status, service date, schedule window, work order, site-readiness and technician notes, customer/member contact posture, privacy constraints, and, when healthcare linked, patient/member status, payer, plan, benefits, eligibility, referral, authorization, order, encounter, and documentation state. Missing authorization, referral, eligibility, agreement status, or technician-readiness evidence blocks completion.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Truth-set systems include EHR/EMR, clinical documentation system, practice management, revenue cycle, payer portal, authorization or utilization management system, referral management, eligibility system, case management, CRM, estimate or quoting system, agreement or e-signature repository, scheduling/dispatch, work-order or FSM platform, technician mobile notes, document repository, privacy/access-control system, and audit logs. Claim truth remains with revenue cycle, coding, payer operations, or utilization management rather than this lane.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical need, medical necessity, care-pathway decisions, licensed review, patient-safety signoff, PHI disclosure approval, consent interpretation, payer coverage, authorization approval, referral sufficiency, claim/coding/denial/appeal decisions, legal agreement interpretation, price approval, refund approval, licensed trade signoff, technician execution, and safety-critical release.

workflow_stages.name: Estimate follow-up intake and boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture requested follow-up action, service model, jurisdiction, estimate record, agreement state, customer or patient/member context, source systems, RACI, escalation SLA, and authority posture before accepting work.
source_classes: Public contractor and home-services consumer guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and evidence validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate estimate version, expected charges, line items, agreement or consent state, CRM notes, schedule window, site-readiness checklist, technician notes, service address, authorization, referral, eligibility, order, encounter, privacy posture, and source-of-truth hierarchy.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Lifecycle execution and follow-up progression
workflow_stages.description: Move accepted work through prerequisites_checked, follow_up_pending, customer_or_member_contacted, agreement_pending, technician_review_needed, blocked, escalated, completed, or returned_to_orchestrator based on auditable evidence and owner acceptance.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public contractor and home-services consumer guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and rework handling
workflow_stages.description: Handle incomplete artifacts, conflicting systems, agreement/scope gaps, schedule or technician blockers, PHI-sensitive communication, payer prerequisites, clinical boundaries, and policy conflicts with explicit owner routing.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Public contractor and home-services consumer guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion confirmation and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Record completion only when estimate disposition, agreement state, required prerequisites, source record links, owner acceptance, residual risks, downstream handoff, and reopen trigger are documented.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: good faith estimate or expected charge line-item summary
agreement, consent, cancellation, or e-signature status
CRM contact log and callback note
work order, scheduling record, dispatch record, and technician mobile note
site-readiness checklist, service address, photos, access notes, infection-control or safety notes, and equipment or parts availability
eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, order, encounter, discharge or care-transition, and clinical-administrative notes
charge capture, coding, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, and payer portal references used only as boundary or routing evidence
privacy-controlled routing note, exception queue note, completion evidence record, and audit log
systems_of_record: technician mobile application or field note repository
EHR or EMR and clinical documentation systems
revenue cycle, charge capture, coding, claim, remittance, denial, or appeal system

allowed: Classify estimate follow-up requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, source links, and owner context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, escalate, or complete the follow-up lifecycle based on documented estimate, agreement, schedule, technician, authorization, referral, eligibility, privacy, and owner evidence.
Prepare field-service follow-up summaries, execution status updates, exception queue notes, escalation notes, and completion evidence records.
escalate: Required estimate, agreement, schedule, technician, EHR/EMR, payer, authorization, referral, eligibility, privacy, or tenant authority records are missing or materially conflict.

The request requires clinical judgment, medical necessity, payer interpretation, PHI disclosure approval, coding, claim, denial, appeal, agreement/legal interpretation, price approval, refund approval, licensed trade signoff, technician execution, or safety-critical release.
Public guidance, tenant policy, payer rules, state cancellation posture, agreement terms, or system records conflict materially.
A patient-safety, PHI, clinical, site-safety, or service-readiness signal changes whether follow-up can continue.
refuse: Mark estimate follow-up complete without documented estimate disposition, agreement status, prerequisite evidence, owner acceptance, and record links.
Approve discounts, refunds, agreement waivers, cancellation outcomes, payer coverage, authorization approval, medical necessity, coding, claim handling, PHI disclosure, or clinical recommendations.
Authorize field service when technician, licensed trade, safety, infection-control, scope, access, or site-readiness blockers require accountable owner review.

domain_failure_modes: estimate follow-up proceeds without authorization, referral, eligibility, agreement, or service-date prerequisites
patient/member PHI is included in broad estimate communications without minimum-necessary controls
the lane implies medical necessity, diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical need, or payer coverage while only owning administrative follow-up
agreement, cancellation, discount, refund, or scope exceptions are approved by the follow-up lane without legal or financial authority
technician readiness, site access, infection-control, safety, or licensed trade blockers are treated as ordinary callback friction
conflicting CRM, estimate, agreement, dispatch, technician, EHR, or payer records are synthesized into an unsupported completion
stale estimates are closed without customer/member disposition, source links, owner acceptance, residual risk, or reopen trigger
tenant terminology is mapped casually and changes the meaning of GFE, authorization, referral, agreement hold, or technician review

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: The queue path is home-services-and-field-consumer-services while the resolved industry profile and required research pack are healthcare-and-care-delivery.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack treats home/field estimate follow-up as the named specialty and applies healthcare safeguards when the tenant service is care-adjacent, DME/HME, home health, payer-funded, or patient/member linked. Runtime must retrieve tenant service model before applying healthcare-only or contractor-only posture.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State contractor, cancellation, home improvement, DME/HME, Medicaid, payer, and agreement rules may vary materially by jurisdiction and tenant contract.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The validated public baseline records the conflict trigger and requires tenant, jurisdiction, payer, agreement, legal, and compliance owner review before jurisdiction-specific or payer-specific action.

notes: The research baseline intentionally covers workflow, evidence, terminology, privacy, clinical/payer boundaries, agreement handling, technician readiness, and failure behavior rather than only quote follow-up.

There is no blocking unresolved ambiguity for a validated specification-based package because the lane refuses or escalates wherever tenant, payer, legal, privacy, clinical, or technician authority is required.
